nodes.name: City
nodes.summary: The city where the lead levels are exceeding federal action thresholds in residential taps.

nodes.name: City Council
nodes.summary: The council that has issued a boil-water advisory and announced an emergency infrastructure replacement program.

nodes.name: Residential Taps
nodes.summary: The taps in the city's older neighborhoods where lead levels are exceeding federal action thresholds.

nodes.name: Low-income Residents
nodes.summary: The residents who are disproportionately affected by the lead levels in the city's older neighborhoods.

nodes.name: Older Neighborhoods
nodes.summary: The neighborhoods where the lead levels are exceeding federal action thresholds in residential taps.

nodes.name: Emergency Infrastructure Replacement Program
nodes.summary: The program announced by the city council to replace the infrastructure in the affected zones.

nodes.name: Community Groups
nodes.summary: The groups that are organizing legal action against the city.

nodes.name: Mayor
nodes.summary: The mayor who has acknowledged the failure of routine testing procedures and announced the resignation of the public works director.

nodes.name: Public Works Director
nodes.summary: The director who has resigned due to the failure of routine testing procedures.

nodes.name: Older Housing Stock
nodes.summary: The housing stock in the city's older neighborhoods that correlates with income levels in the city.
